title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: People believe that the painting is worth millions.
‘You stole my idea!’ the scientist said to his colleague.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verb 'accuse' + object + of + gerund.

questions.firstSentence: The only reason I passed was because of your encouragement.
questions.afterGap: your encouragement, I wouldn't have passed.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional fixed phrase 'If it hadn't been for'.

questions.firstSentence: Perhaps they didn't receive the invitation in time.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for past possibility/uncertainty.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't realize my phone was missing until I got home.
They are similar in many ways, but they are not identical.
questions.afterGap: them, though they are not identical.
questions.explanation: Tests noun phrases and preposition usage.
